briefdescription.para: PCAP CR Register.
Write data to the Write FIFO.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.
is the 32-bit value to be written to the FIFO.

simplesect.para: C-style Signature: void XHwIcap_FifoWrite(XHwIcap *InstancePtr, u32 Data);

briefdescription.para: Read data from the Read FIFO.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.

simplesect.para: The 32-bit Data read from the FIFO.

briefdescription.para: Set the number of words to be read from the Icap in the Size register.

detaileddescription.para: The Size Register holds the number of 32 bit words to transfer from the the Icap to the Read FIFO of the HwIcap device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.
is the size in words.

simplesect.para: C-style Signature: void XHwIcap_SetSizeReg(XHwIcap *InstancePtr, u32 Data);

briefdescription.para: Get the contents of the Control register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.

simplesect.para: A 32-bit value representing the contents of the Control register.

briefdescription.para: Set the Control Register to initiate a configuration (write) to the device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.

briefdescription.para: Set the Control Register to initiate a ReadBack from the device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.

briefdescription.para: Get the contents of the status register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.

simplesect.para: A 32-bit value representing the contents of the status register.

briefdescription.para: This macro checks if the last Read/Write of the data to the Read/Write FIFO of the HwIcap device is completed.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.

listitem.para: TRUE if the Read/Write to the FIFO's is completed.
FALSE if the Read/Write to the FIFO's is NOT completed..

briefdescription.para: This macro checks if the last Read/Write to the ICAP device in the FPGA is completed.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.

listitem.para: TRUE if the last Read/Write(Config) to the ICAP is NOT completed.
FALSE if the Read/Write(Config) to the ICAP is completed..

briefdescription.para: This macro enables the global interrupt in the Global Interrupt Enable Register (GIER) so that the interrupt output from the HwIcap device is enabled.

detaileddescription.para: Interrupts enabled using XHwIcap_IntrEnable() will not occur until the global interrupt enable bit is set by using this macro.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HwIcap instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro disables the global interrupt in the Global Interrupt Enable Register (GIER) so that the interrupt output from the HwIcap device is disabled.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HwIcap instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro returns the interrupt status read from Interrupt Status Register(IPISR).

detaileddescription.para: Use the XHI_IPIXR_* constants defined in xhwicap_l.h to interpret the returned value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HwIcap instance.

simplesect.para: The contents read from the Interrupt Status Register.

briefdescription.para: This macro disables the specified interrupts in the Interrupt Enable Register.

detaileddescription.para: It is non-destructive in that the register is read and only the interrupts specified is changed.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HwIcap instance.

is the bit-mask of the interrupts to be disabled. Bit positions of 1 will be disabled. Bit positions of 0 will keep the previous setting. This mask is formed by OR'ing XHI_IPIXR_*_MASK bits defined in xhwicap_l.h.

briefdescription.para: This macro enables the specified interrupts in the Interrupt Enable Register.

detaileddescription.para: It is non-destructive in that the register is read and only the interrupts specified is changed.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HwIcap instance.

is the bit-mask of the interrupts to be enabled. Bit positions of 1 will be enabled. Bit positions of 0 will keep the previous setting. This mask is formed by OR'ing XHI_IPIXR_*_MASK bits defined in xhwicap_l.h.

briefdescription.para: This macro returns the interrupt status read from Interrupt Enable Register(IIER).

detaileddescription.para: Use the XHI_IPIXR_* constants defined in xhwicap_l.h to interpret the returned value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HwIcap instance.

simplesect.para: The contents read from the Interrupt Enable Register.

briefdescription.para: This macro clears the specified interrupts in the Interrupt Status Register (IPISR).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HwIcap instance.
contains the interrupts to be cleared.

briefdescription.para: This macro returns the vacancy of the Write FIFO.

detaileddescription.para: This indicates the number of words that can be written to the Write FIFO before it becomes full.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HwIcap instance.

simplesect.para: The contents read from the Write FIFO Vacancy Register.

briefdescription.para: This macro returns the occupancy of the Read FIFO.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HwIcap instance.

simplesect.para: The contents read from the Read FIFO Occupancy Register.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* XHwIcap_StatusHandler)(void *CallBackRef, u32 StatusEvent, u32 WordCount)
memberdef.name: XHwIcap_StatusHandler

briefdescription.para: The handler data type allows the user to define a callback function to handle the asynchronous processing of the HwIcap driver.

detaileddescription.para: The application using this driver is expected to define a handler of this type to support interrupt driven mode. The handler executes in an interrupt context such that minimal processing should be performed.

parameterdescription.para: is a callback reference passed in by the application layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked. Its type is unimportant to the driver component, so it is a void pointer.
indicates one or more status events that occurred. See the XHwIcap_SetInterruptHandler for details on the status events that can be passed in the callback.
indicates how many words of data were successfully transferred. This may be less than the number of words requested if there was an error.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes a specific XHwIcap instance.

detaileddescription.para: The IDCODE is read from the FPGA and based on the IDCODE the information about the resources in the FPGA is filled in the instance structure.
The HwIcap device will be in put in a reset state before exiting this function.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.
points to the XHwIcap device configuration structure.

is the device base address in the virtual memory address space. If the address translation is not used then the physical address is passed. Unexpected errors may occur if the address mapping is changed after this function is invoked.

briefdescription.para: This function writes the given user data to the Write FIFO in both the polled mode and the interrupt mode and starts the transfer of the data to the ICAP device.

detaileddescription.para: In the polled mode, this function will write the specified number of words into the FIFO before returning.

In the interrupt mode, this function will write the words up to the size of the Write FIFO and starts the transfer, then subsequent transfer of the data is performed by the interrupt service routine until the entire buffer has been transferred. The status callback function is called when the entire buffer has been sent. In order to use interrupts, it is necessary for the user to connect the driver interrupt handler, XHwIcap_IntrHandler(), to the interrupt system of the application and enable the interrupts associated with the Write FIFO. The user has to enable the interrupts each time this function is called using the XHwIcap_IntrEnable macro.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.
is a pointer to the data to be written to the ICAP device.
is the number of words (16 bit for S6 and 32 bit for all other devices)to write to the ICAP device.

simplesect.para: This function is a blocking for the polled mode of operation and is non-blocking for the interrupt mode of operation. Use the function XHwIcap_DeviceWriteFrame for writing a frame of data to the ICAP device.

briefdescription.para: This function reads the specified number of words from the ICAP device in the polled mode.

detaileddescription.para: Interrupt mode is not supported in reading data from the ICAP device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.
is a pointer to the memory where the frame read from the ICAP device is stored.
is the number of words (16 bit for S6 and 32 bit for all other devices) to write to the ICAP device.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the specified number of words have been read from the ICAP device
XST_FAILURE if the device is busy with the last Read/Write or if the requested number of words have not been read from the ICAP device, or there is a timeout.

briefdescription.para: This function forces the software reset of the complete HWICAP device.

detaileddescription.para: All the registers will return to the default value and the FIFO is also flushed as a part of this software reset.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.

briefdescription.para: This function flushes the FIFOs in the device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.

briefdescription.para: This function initiates the Abort Sequence by setting the Abort bit in the control register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.

briefdescription.para: Looks up the device configuration based on the unique device ID.

detaileddescription.para: A table contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: contains the ID of the device for which the device configuration pointer is to be returned.

listitem.para: A pointer to the configuration found.
NULL if the specified device ID was not found.

briefdescription.para: Sends a DESYNC command to the ICAP port.

parameterdescription.para: - a pointer to the XHwIcap instance to be worked on

briefdescription.para: Sends a CAPTURE command to the ICAP port.

detaileddescription.para: This command captures all of the flip flop states so they will be available during readback. One can use this command instead of enabling the CAPTURE block in the design.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the value of the specified configuration register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.
is a constant which represents the configuration register value to be returned. Constants specified in xhwicap_i.h. Examples: XHI_IDCODE, XHI_FLR.
is the value of the specified configuration register.

briefdescription.para: Run a self-test on the driver/device.

detaileddescription.para:
The test
Writes to the Interrupt Enable Register and reads it back for comparison.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the value read from the register is the same as the value written.

briefdescription.para: The interrupt handler for HwIcap interrupts.

detaileddescription.para: This function must be connected by the user to an interrupt source.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.

simplesect.para: The interrupts are being used only while writing data to the ICAP device. The reading of the data from the ICAP device is done in polled mode. In a worst case scenario the interrupt handler can be busy writing large amount of data to the Write FIFO.

briefdescription.para: Sets the status callback function, the status handler, which the driver calls when it encounters conditions that should be reported to the higher layer software.

detaileddescription.para: The handler executes in an interrupt context, so it must minimize the amount of processing performed such as transferring data to a thread context. One of the following status events is passed to the status handler.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.
CallBackRef
is the upper layer callback reference passed back when the callback function is invoked.
FuncPtr
is the pointer to the callback function.
None.
The handler is called within interrupt context, so it should do its work quickly and queue potentially time-consuming work to a task-level thread.

memberdef.argsstring: (XHwIcap *InstancePtr, long Top, long Block, long HClkRow, long MajorFrame, long MinorFrame, u32 *FrameBuffer)
memberdef.name: XHwIcap_DeviceReadFrame

briefdescription.para: Reads one frame from the device and puts it in memory specified by the user.

parameterdescription.para: - a pointer to the XHwIcap instance to be worked on.
- top (0) or bottom (1) half of device
- Block Address (XHI_FAR_CLB_BLOCK, XHI_FAR_BRAM_BLOCK, XHI_FAR_BRAM_INT_BLOCK)
- selects the HClk Row
- selects the column
- selects frame inside column
is a pointer to the memory where the frame read from the device is stored

memberdef.argsstring: (XHwIcap *InstancePtr, long Top, long Block, long HClkRow, long MajorFrame, long MinorFrame, u32 *FrameData)
memberdef.name: XHwIcap_DeviceWriteFrame

briefdescription.para: Writes one frame from the specified buffer and puts it in the device (ICAP).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHwIcap instance.
- top (0) or bottom (1) half of device
- Block Address (XHI_FAR_CLB_BLOCK, XHI_FAR_BRAM_BLOCK, XHI_FAR_BRAM_INT_BLOCK)
- selects the HClk Row
- selects the column
- selects frame inside column
is a pointer to the frame that is to be written to the device.

simplesect.para: This is a blocking function. This function is used in conjunction with the function XHwIcap_DeviceReadFrame. This function is used to write back the frame of data read using the XHwIcap_DeviceReadFrame.
